exampleSentences: Ổng nói là hôm nay sẽ đến nhà anh chơi.
Sáng nay ổng đi làm rất sớm vì có cuộc họp quan trọng.
Nếu ổng đồng ý, em sẽ tham gia dự án cùng ổng.
notes:
Ổng = “he,” but casual and Southern. It comes from ông ấy (that man).

Used to talk about a man you and the listener both know. Depending on tone, it can feel neutral, joking, or slightly dismissive.

Mostly used in Southern Vietnamese; in the North people usually say ông ấy instead.

Close to ảnh (anh ấy), but ổng is for an older man or someone you refer to as ông, while ảnh is for someone you call anh.

definitions.es: Un término utilizado en vietnamita para referirse a un hombre mayor, a menudo usado como una forma respetuosa de dirigirse a un padre o abuelo.
definitions.vi: Một thuật ngữ được sử dụng trong tiếng Việt để chỉ một người đàn ông lớn tuổi, thường được dùng như một cách tôn trọng để gọi cha hoặc ông.
definitions.en: A term used in Vietnamese to refer to an older man, often used as a respectful way to address a father or grandfather.

example.es: Él es un hombre muy bueno.
example.vi: Ông ấy là người rất tốt.
example.en: He is a very good man.

exampleSentencesTranslated: He said that he will come to your house today.
This morning he went to work very early because he had an important meeting.
If he agrees, I will join the project with him.
notes_md:
**Ổng** = “he,” but casual and Southern. It comes from **ông ấy** (that man).

Used to talk about a man you and the listener both know. Depending on tone, it can feel neutral, joking, or slightly dismissive.

Mostly used in Southern Vietnamese; in the North people usually say **ông ấy** instead.

Close to **ảnh** (anh ấy), but **ổng** is for an older man or someone you refer to as *ông*, while **ảnh** is for someone you call *anh*.
